attributes.content:
## Overview

The Vehicle card gives the workspace a structured home for vehicle record: make, model, year, VIN, mileage. Links to service records, insurance, and mileage log.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Vehicle acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Calendar]]** -- Calendar can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Vehicle.
- **[[Task]]** -- Vehicle often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Alert]]** -- Alert can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Vehicle.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Vehicle can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Document]]** -- Vehicle can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Recipe]]** -- Recipe can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Vehicle.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Vehicle as the structured home for vehicle record: make, model, year, VIN, mileage. Links to service records, insurance, and mileage log and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Vehicle when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Vehicle stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
